channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: Iran Reviewing US Peace Proposal as Trump Warns of Further Strikes
item.description: Tehran is reviewing Washington's latest response to a ceasefire proposal, with Trump saying negotiations are in final stages but threatening renewed attacks if no deal is reached.

item.title: Germany Proposes Associate EU Membership for Ukraine
item.description: Chancellor Friedrich Merz has proposed giving Ukraine a direct role in EU structures as an interim step toward full membership, potentially helping facilitate a deal to end the war with Russia.

item.title: Russia Delivers Nuclear Munitions to Belarus for Drills
item.description: Russia has delivered nuclear munitions to field storage facilities in Belarus as part of major nuclear exercises, escalating tensions with the West over Ukraine.

item.title: Russian Oil and Gas Revenue Surges 39% Due to Iran War Price Rally
item.description: Russia's state oil and gas revenues are projected to rise 39% year-on-year in May to 700 billion roubles, driven by global oil price increases from the Iran conflict.

item.title: Central Russian Oil Refineries Halt Output After Ukrainian Drone Strikes
item.description: Major oil refineries in central Russia have been forced to stop or reduce fuel production following Ukrainian drone attacks, according to official data and industry sources.

item.title: Ukraine Strikes Major Russian Oil Refinery in Syzran
item.description: Ukrainian drones hit Rosneft's Syzran refinery in Samara Oblast, 700km from Ukraine, killing two. All central Russia's major refineries have halted or reduced production following recent Ukrainian strikes.

item.title: Japan Dismisses Russia-China Claims of Remilitarization
item.description: Tokyo rejected a joint statement by Presidents Xi and Putin accusing Japan of accelerating remilitarization. The dispute comes as Japan strengthens defense ties with South Korea amid China's growing influence.

item.title: Multiple Nations Summon Israeli Envoys Over Flotilla Activist Treatment
item.description: France, Netherlands, Canada, Ireland, Spain, Italy, Germany, Greece, Britain, Turkey and New Zealand summoned Israeli ambassadors over National Security Minister Ben-Gvir's video showing detained Gaza flotilla activists kneeling and tied. Netanyahu criticized the minister's actions.

item.title: Rare Ebola strain spreads rapidly in eastern Congo as health workers struggle
item.description: The Bundibugyo virus, a rare type of Ebola, has spread undetected for weeks in eastern Congo, with 139 suspected deaths and 600 cases. WHO says cases may exceed 1,000, with a vaccine months away amid conflict zones and underfunded health systems.

item.title: Far-right Israeli minister Ben-Gvir condemned for taunting handcuffed Gaza flotilla activists
item.description: National Security Minister Itamar Ben-Gvir posted video showing himself telling detained pro-Palestinian activists "We are the masters" while they knelt bound. The footage drew condemnation from the US, UK, Canada, France, Italy, Australia and others, with Netanyahu calling it "not in line with Israel's values."

item.title: US military surveillance flights track near Cuba as tensions mount
item.description: BBC Verify analysis shows at least five US Navy P-8A Poseidon jets and three MQ-4C Triton drones have operated near Cuba since May 11, with flights as close as 50 miles from the island. Experts say the visible flight transponders are likely a deliberate signal to enforce the oil blockade and deter Venezuela.

item.title: Austrian ex-intelligence officer convicted of spying for Russia
item.description: Egisto Ott, 63, was sentenced to four years for passing classified information to Russian intelligence and Wirecard fugitive Jan Marsalek. The trial revealed Ott copied phones from Austrian interior ministry officials after they fell into the Danube, reviving concerns about Russian espionage in Austria.

item.title: Xi and Putin Conclude Beijing Summit Without Finalizing Gas Pipeline Deal
item.description: Chinese and Russian leaders wrapped up talks in Beijing with an "understanding" on the long-stalled Power of Siberia 2 gas pipeline but no final agreement. The summit came days after Xi hosted Trump, highlighting China's balancing act between major powers.

item.title: Xi Jinping May Visit North Korea as Early as Next Week
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping may visit North Korea as early as next week in what would be his first trip to the country in seven years. The visit comes amid efforts to strengthen bilateral ties and potential mediation between Trump and Kim Jong Un.

item.title: Xi Warned Trump That Taiwan Differences Could Lead to Conflict
item.description: During their summit, Chinese President Xi Jinping warned Trump that differences over Taiwan could lead to conflict between the two powers. The discussion highlights Taiwan as a critical flashpoint in US-China relations.

item.title: Hezbollah Conducts 24 Operations Against Israeli Forces in 24 Hours
item.description: The group claims attacks included drone swarms, Merkava tank strikes, and direct confrontations with Israeli troops, escalating border conflict intensity.

item.title: Putin's Trolls Weakening Merkel's Successor to Boost Russia-Friendly Far Right
item.description: As German Chancellor Merz struggles domestically and the AfD rises ahead of key elections, Russian disinformation campaigns are intensifying to influence German politics.

item.title: Australia to Begin Construction on Major Rare Earth Project in September
item.description: Arafura Resources, backed by Gina Rinehart and the federal government, will build Australia's first ore-to-oxide rare earth processing facility, reducing Western dependence on Chinese supply chains.

item.title: Australian Court Upholds $465,000 Fine Against Musk's X Over Child Safety
item.description: X Corp admitted violating Australian law by failing to provide information about child protection measures, ending a three-year legal battle with the eSafety commissioner.

item.title: US Gasoline Prices to Rise as Iran War Disrupts Summer Travel
item.description: High gasoline prices fueled by supply disruptions from the Iran conflict are set to add financial strain for US motorists during the Memorial Day weekend, kicking off a costlier summer travel season.

item.title: US Racing to Develop Ebola Treatment for Congo Outbreak
item.description: The US government is working with a small biotech firm on an experimental treatment for the Bundibugyo Ebola strain causing the deadly outbreak in Congo, for which no approved vaccine exists.

item.title: Xi and Putin Wrap Beijing Talks with No Deal on Key Gas Pipeline
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin left Beijing with no breakthrough on a major natural gas pipeline project, though the two leaders signed over 40 cooperation agreements and jointly criticized US policies.

item.title: UK Inflation Cools More Than Expected in April
item.description: British inflation fell more than expected in April, but the slowdown offers only temporary relief as global costs from the Iran war are expected to hit households harder later this year.

item.title: US Indicts Former Cuban President Raúl Castro Over 1996 Plane Shootdown
item.description: Federal prosecutors announced charges against former Cuban President Raúl Castro for his role in the 1996 downing of civilian planes operated by Miami-based exiles, escalating US pressure on Cuba. Trump said no escalation is expected.

item.title: NATO Chief Rutte Says US Troop Withdrawal From Europe Will Not Harm Defense
item.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte stated that planned US troop withdrawals from Europe will unfold gradually in a structured way and will not impact NATO defense plans, as Europe moves to strengthen its own defense capabilities.
